catalysts_general: Discovery: Lamb Weston is a real-business turnaround with a $5.87 billion market cap, positive net income, and a 36.99% drawdown from the 52-week high while trading just 6.72% above the low; the timing trigger is April 1, 2026 earnings plus Starboard's March 9 activist push for deeper cost cuts and strategic action, while the bear case is that frozen-potato demand and leverage stay too weak for activism to matter quickly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap, cash-generative frozen-potato leader with a credible activist-backed self-help story after April 1 results lifted the FY2026 sales and EBITDA outlook, but the upside still depends on margin repair rather than strong end-demand.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom remains one of the highest-conviction AI infrastructure beneficiaries after posting record March-quarter results, triple-digit AI revenue growth, and much stronger-than-expected forward guidance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Record AI semiconductor growth, strong Q2 guidance, and ongoing AI-networking product ramps keep Broadcom attractive despite rich valuation and VMware overhangs.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Profitable mega-cap software compounder now sits 36.95% below its 52-week high and just 6.93% above its low despite 9.58% trailing sales growth and 17.96% profit margins; the why-now trigger is management commencing a $25 billion accelerated share repurchase on March 16, 2026 with the next earnings report estimated for May 27, 2026, though the bear case is that AI pressure and decelerating seat growth keep the multiple compressed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce is a cash-rich, still-growing software leader trading at a compressed ~12x forward earnings multiple while management is executing a historic $25 billion ASR and insiders bought stock into the March pullback.

Microsoft combines mega-cap quality with still-strong Azure and Copilot growth, and the April 29 earnings report plus May-June AI product milestones keep the setup constructive after a 33% pullback from the high.
FIS looks like a cash-generative fintech rerating candidate after the Issuer Solutions acquisition and Worldpay exit, but the upside depends on integration and deleveraging execution.
Single-digit forward earnings multiple and strong free cash flow make CROX a contrarian buy if May earnings confirm HEYDUDE stabilization and Crocs-brand margin resilience.

catalysts_general: Discovery: UNH is still roughly 55% below its 52-week high and only about 15% above its low, with the April 6, 2026 final Medicare Advantage rate notice and April 21, 2026 earnings as real stop-the-bleeding catalysts, though utilization pressure and policy scrutiny can still push estimates lower.
growth_picks.analyst_take: UNH is a washed-out, cash-generative franchise trading on a compressed forward multiple, and the April CMS rate notice plus April earnings can stabilize estimates if management shows utilization and pricing discipline are improving.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Jeff Green's roughly $148 million open-market purchase after the post-earnings collapse is one of the clearest insider-conviction signals in large-cap software this quarter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TTD looks like a high-quality ad-tech franchise mispriced after its collapse: Jeff Green bought roughly 6.0 million shares in early March 2026, while official company releases still point to durable retention, strong margins, and new CTV/OpenAds catalysts, though agency-transparency disputes keep this out of STRONG BUY territory.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Roper has been hit with a textbook capitulation unwind, falling 40.65% from its 52-week high and only 12.42% above its low even though it still earns $1.54 billion with 28.29% operating margins; the catalyst is the next Q1 earnings check later in April 2026 after a cluster of January downgrades at the lows, but the bear case is that organic growth has structurally slowed and the market is right to rerate the acquisitive model.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality vertical software compounder now looks reasonably priced after a 40% drawdown, with strong cash generation and buyback/M&A optionality offsetting a slower Deltek-led growth outlook.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sprinklr is a disliked small-cap software turnaround, but the March earnings beat, sizeable new buyback, and still-depressed valuation make it a credible re-rating candidate.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cash-rich small-cap software turnaround with a newly authorized buyback and solid free cash flow, but the rerating depends on proving growth can reaccelerate from low-single digits.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Barchart flagged Unity's April 24, 2026 $23 calls with 14,500 contracts traded versus just 146 open interest on March 31, 2026, roughly $2.03 million of premium, days after Unity pre-announced Q1 revenue and EBITDA well above guidance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Unity's March preliminary Q1 beat and exit from non-strategic ad assets strengthen the turnaround, but high volatility and competitive pressure keep the setup speculative.
Lifyorli's March 25 FDA approval gives Corcept a second commercial engine beyond Korlym, but generic and regulatory overhangs keep this from high-conviction territory.
Deeply discounted CX outsourcer with maintained FY2026 profit and FCF targets, active buybacks, and recent refinancing support, but the rebound case depends on Q2 margin and cash-flow stabilization.
Duolingo reset from a premium multiple into a profitable, debt-light cash generator, and the post-guidance washout looks buyable if April earnings show the 2026 user-growth pivot is holding.
H&R Block looks mispriced at roughly 7x trailing earnings and an 11% FCF yield if the May 6, 2026 tax-season print confirms stable filing execution and capital returns.

Workday looks like a profitable, cash-rich software rerating candidate after a 54% drawdown, but the bull case depends on Sana and broader agentic AI adoption reaccelerating subscription growth after a soft FY27 guide.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Denali just converted a long-watched FDA event into a real approval for Avlayah, creating a fresh commercial and platform-validation narrative after years of development risk.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FDA approval of AVLAYAH on March 25, 2026 de-risks Denali's lead asset and validates its blood-brain-barrier platform, while fresh Royalty Pharma cash supports launch execution, though confirmatory and commercialization risk keep conviction below high.

NVIDIA still looks like the highest-quality AI infrastructure franchise in the market, and the combination of record fiscal 2026 results, a fresh Rubin product cycle and a reasonable forward multiple supports upside, but China restrictions and custom-silicon competition keep this out of STRONG BUY territory.
High-quality ad compounder with strong 2025 growth and cash generation, but the 2026 AI capex step-up and legal overhang keep this at BUY rather than STRONG BUY.

FDA approval of KRESLADI and the attached rare pediatric voucher materially de-risk Rocket, but the commercial launch is slow, the addressable market is tiny, and financing overhang still limits upside.
APP still has elite growth, margins, cash flow, and strong Q1 guidance, but the active SEC probe and concentrated March insider selling keep the setup from clearing a clean BUY bar.
Spin-off and debt cleanup can unlock value, but post-spin comparability is messy and tariff-driven auto demand risk keeps the risk/reward short of a fresh buy.

FDA approval in acquired hypothalamic obesity gives Rhythm a real new revenue leg, but the stock still prices in a lot for a cash-burning biotech after the EMANATE miss and without a clean insider-buying signal.

STERIS is a high-quality infection-prevention franchise with maintained 8-9% FY26 revenue growth guidance and rising free cash flow, but the stock is still not cheap enough to offset higher tariff pressure, litigation risk, and the lack of bullish insider buying.

Nike remains a cash-generative global brand and Q3 showed some wholesale stabilization, but China deterioration, tariff-driven margin pressure, and no bullish insider accumulation keep the turnaround short of a BUY.

Domino's remains a high-quality, cash-generative franchise compounder, but after the February 23, 2026 results the near-term setup looks balanced because 2026 same-store-sales expectations are modest and recent insider activity was selling, not buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista's March 12 XPO optics announcement points to another leg of AI-cluster scaling, where liquid-cooled optical interconnect density becomes a hard constraint and Arista keeps monetizing the network layer around AI factories.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Best-in-class AI Ethernet and cloud networking execution supports continued growth, but ANET still trades at a premium multiple with meaningful hyperscaler concentration and no insider-buying confirmation.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Constellation remains one of the cleanest public ways to express institutional rotation into AI-power and nuclear-backed electricity scarcity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality nuclear-and-gas power platform with real AI/data-center demand tailwinds and Calpine-driven cash flow, but the valuation is still rich and merger-related supply/execution risks limit near-term upside.

catalysts_general: Discovery: United Therapeutics printed a fresh biotech catalyst with late-March TETON-1 success, adding meaningful upside optionality beyond its already cash-generative pulmonary franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality, cash-rich pulmonary franchise with real 2026 pipeline catalysts, but the stock is already near its 52-week high and the risk/reward is only average after the TETON-1 rerating.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO said on March 26, 2026 that European allies and Canada lifted defense spending 20% year over year and are now moving toward a 5% of GDP target, which directly extends demand for Raytheon's Patriot air-defense systems and interceptor replenishment across Europe.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has real backlog and defense-demand support, but at roughly 9% below its 52-week high the stock already prices in much of the good news while Pratt execution risk still hangs over the story.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anastasios Arima bought about $1.79M, director Todd Hannigan $720.9K, president Toby Symonds $262.6K and director Tony Tripeny $99.5K on March 27, 2026 after a roughly 48% one-month drawdown, a four-person cluster that reads as high-conviction dip buying.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IperionX has credible U.S. government-backed titanium supply-chain catalysts and fresh insider cluster buying, but the stock still hinges on proving commercial scale and converting prototypes into repeat revenue.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Cisco has crossed from AI narrative to AI revenue, with February Q2 results showing $2.1 billion of hyperscaler AI infrastructure orders and March launches extending Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA plus 102.4 Tbps Silicon One switching into edge and enterprise deployments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cisco is converting AI networking demand into real orders and solid earnings growth, but the stock is no longer cheap enough to justify a fresh buy given limited upside, supply-cost pressure, and neutral insider signals.

catalysts_general: Discovery: BORR logged a two-director buying cluster on March 24, 2026, with Tor Olav Troim buying about $2.60 million and Jeffrey Currie buying about $1.33 million, a $3.93 million combined vote of confidence in a beaten-down offshore driller.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Heavy insider buying and improving jack-up market visibility support the floor, but BORR is already near its 52-week high and still carries meaningful leverage, customer-payment, and geopolitical risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Samsara's March 5 results showed AI features translating into enterprise spend, with ARR near $1.9 billion and record $1 million-plus ACV deals, which is the kind of monetized industrial-AI adoption most software names still lack.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Samsara is executing well with 30% ARR growth, positive free cash flow, and tangible AI product monetization, but the stock still carries a premium multiple and a founder-sale overhang that limit near-term upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PYPL sits roughly 43% below its 52-week high and only about 18% above its low, with Apr. 28, 2026 earnings and the March 17, 2026 expansion of PYUSD to 70 markets as concrete near-term catalysts, though checkout growth and stablecoin monetization still need to prove they are big enough to matter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PYPL is statistically cheap and highly cash-generative, but the market is waiting for proof that branded checkout can stabilize under new leadership before rewarding the turnaround.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Japan-backed US power projects now include up to $33 billion of natural-gas generation in Pennsylvania and Texas operated by NextEra Energy Resources, creating a policy-linked domestic buildout catalyst.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NextEra still has credible long-term growth from FPL rate-base expansion and AI/data-center power demand, but at roughly 2.5% below its 52-week high with thin target upside, negative free cash flow and fresh equity financing, the setup looks like a quality compounder that is already priced for much of the good news.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Benzinga's options scanner flagged a bullish May 15, 2026 $120 put sweep in Citigroup totaling about $2.3M across 2,458 contracts, an unusually large premium outlay that implies institutions are leaning constructive and willing to own stock near the strike.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Citi is still inexpensive and has real near-term catalysts, but after rerating to within about 7% of its 52-week high the easy upside is gone and card-credit plus execution risk still cap the setup.

catalysts_general: Discovery: SMCI remains roughly 63% below its 52-week high and less than 20% above its low, with May 5, 2026 earnings as the hard catalyst for AI-server demand and margin stabilization, but export-control and governance headlines keep this firmly in higher-risk territory.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI-server demand is still real and valuation is compressed, but unresolved export-control/governance risk and severe margin pressure keep SMCI below buy quality for now.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Grocery Outlet has seen repeated open-market purchases by the CEO and multiple directors near 52-week lows, which is exactly the kind of cluster buying that can mark a washout bottom.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider cluster buying and a new CEO create a credible turnaround setup, but the 36-store optimization plan, weak 2026 guidance, and negative free cash flow mean the reset still needs proof before capital should chase it.
High-margin HCM franchise with strong Q3 execution, Paycor integration progress, and a real insider buy, but acquisition-aided growth and limited upside versus risks keep PAYX in hold territory.
High-quality Permian operator with strong free cash flow and buyback support, but shares are near 52-week highs and recent March selling activity plus oil-price sensitivity cap near-term upside.

Gartner is cheap on forward earnings and still throws off cash, but the February 3, 2026 results showed a real slowdown in consulting and contract-value momentum, so the valuation alone is not enough for a fresh buy yet.
AWS and advertising momentum remain strong, but Amazon is not cheap enough for a fresh contrarian buy while 2026 capex and regulatory risk keep near-term upside less compelling.

Snap is executing a credible profitable-growth pivot with buybacks, subscription growth, and a late-2026 Specs launch, but ad-demand pressure, heavy SBC, and legal/regulatory overhang keep the setup short of a clean buy.
Mixed Phase 3 SENTRY data, warrant-heavy financing, and cash runway only into late Q3 2026 leave KPTI as a binary, dilution-prone biotech despite near-term pipeline readouts.

EchoStar has real deleveraging catalysts, but with a going-concern warning, multiple 2026 debt cliffs, negative free cash flow, weakening core subscriber trends, and March insider selling, the stock does not offer a favorable risk/reward near its highs.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Rocket Lab moved onto the radar after landing a record hypersonic-launch contract that strengthens the case for it as a scaled defense-space platform rather than only a small-launch story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rocket Lab is executing well in defense and space systems, but the stock is priced for near-perfect delivery despite negative free cash flow, a delayed Neutron schedule, and fresh dilution risk.
